name: Mwari
description: Mwari is the supreme creator deity in Shona religion and mythology. Often considered transcendent and unknowable, Mwari is believed to be the source of all life and the ultimate authority in the universe. Mwari is not typically depicted in human form and is understood to be a spiritual presence that permeates all things. Communication with Mwari is usually facilitated through spirit mediums and priests, particularly within the Matonjeni shrine in the Matobo Hills, a sacred site central to Shona religious practice. Mwari is associated with rain, fertility, and the well-being of the Shona people. Prayers and offerings are made to Mwari to ensure successful harvests, protection from drought, and overall prosperity. Mwari's influence extends to all aspects of Shona life, from social customs and governance to individual morality and spiritual guidance. The concept of Mwari embodies a complex understanding of the divine, encompassing both immanence and transcendence, and serves as a unifying force within Shona culture. Mwari's role is that of a provider, a judge, and an ultimate protector of the Shona people, with a strong emphasis on ethical conduct and respect for the natural world. Mwari is sometimes referred to by other names, reflecting the multifaceted nature of the deity and the diverse regional variations within Shona religious beliefs. The worship of Mwari has persisted for centuries, demonstrating the enduring significance of this supreme being in Shona spiritual life.
subtitle: The Supreme Creator Deity of the Shona

keyMyths.title: Mwari's Creation
keyMyths.description: Mwari created the world and all living things, breathing life into the earth and establishing the natural order. The exact details of the creation vary, but Mwari's role as the ultimate source is consistent.

keyMyths.title: The Voice from the Rocks
keyMyths.description: Mwari's voice is said to be heard from the rocks of the Matobo Hills, guiding the people and offering solutions to their problems. This emphasizes Mwari's continued involvement in the world.

keyMyths.title: The Drought and the Plea
keyMyths.description: During times of severe drought, the Shona people turn to Mwari with prayers and offerings, seeking rain and relief from suffering. This demonstrates Mwari's power over natural phenomena.
